Back to Conference Notebook

iPullRank · Live Notebook

SEO Week 2026

Apr 26–30, 2026 New York City, NYUpcomingOfficial site

One agenda, thousands of insights. A four-day deep-dive into how AI is rewriting search — covering retrieval engines, the psychology of search, vertical ecosystems (enterprise/local/video), and the future of conversational platforms.

Days5
Sessions61
Speakers39
Hours32
Agenda Browse Speakers · 39

Agenda · 4-Day Timeline

Jump to

Sunday, April 26, 2026

Pre-conference

Early registration and badge pickup before the main program kicks off Monday.

3:00 PM

5:00 PM

registration

Early Registration

Stop by the venue to pick up your badge, get oriented, and avoid the morning rush before sessions begin.

Monday, April 27, 2026

Day 01 · The Science

From crawling and indexing to large language models and retrieval engines — the technical foundations of search and AI, how they work, how they're evolving, and how SEOs can stay ahead of the machines shaping discovery.

8:00 AM

8:45 AM

registration

Registration & Coffee

8:45 AM

9:00 AM

keynote

Welcome & Opening Remarks

Mike King

Founder & CEO, iPullRank

Quick welcome, key housekeeping, and a look at what's ahead.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

9:00 AM

9:45 AM

keynote

The Invisible, Converged Web: Architecting Visibility for the AI Economy

Krishna Madhavan

Principal Product Manager, Microsoft AI

The web is shifting from pages and blue-link rankings to an invisible, converged information layer. Visibility is no longer about where content shows up — it's about whether text, images, video, code, and structured data can be understood, trusted, and reused inside modern AI pipelines.

  • →Map the hidden layers: grounding, safety, attribution checks
  • →Multimodal visibility playbook for AI systems
  • →Designing how robots.txt + signals shape the entire AI journey
My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

9:45 AM

10:15 AM

talk

Beyond the Million-Token Window: TurboQuant and RLM-on-KG

Andrea Volpini

Co-founder & CEO, WordLift

Limits of infinite context, context rot, and why staged retrieval over knowledge graphs (RLM-on-KG) beats bigger windows alone.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

10:15 AM

10:45 AM

break

Morning Break

10:45 AM

11:30 AM

talk

F*CK it, I'll Do it Myself

Mike King

Founder & CEO, iPullRank

Burn the playbook: SEO tools are mostly keyword math with a ChatGPT wrapper. Build real relevance systems yourself with open source and automation.

  • →Why 'AI-powered SEO tools' are mostly cosplay
  • →What relevance engineering actually looks like
  • →Build + automate the stuff vendors won't ship
My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

11:30 AM

12:00 PM

talk

Build the Tool Your Team Actually Needs: A Product Thinking Playbook for SEOs

Noah Learner

Director of Innovation, Sterling Sky

How Noah built SEOLoop with AI and no dev team. Not a demo — a playbook for spotting the process that should be software, scoping it for AI, and shipping.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

12:00 PM

1:30 PM

meal

Lunch

1:30 PM

2:00 PM

talk

From Vibes to Veteran: 7 Tips to Disaster-Proof Your Code

Annie Cushing

Founder, Annielytics

Speed without discipline creates fragility. Seven hard-earned lessons from building real apps with AI to move fast AND bulletproof your code.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

2:00 PM

2:30 PM

talk

Prioritize Investments by Financial Impact

Dale Bertrand

President, Fire&Spark

The new GEO playbook rewards revenue-first marketers. Replace traffic reporting with revenue-first GEO metrics; connect AI search performance to CFO-level ROI.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

2:30 PM

3:00 PM

talk

HEO: The Hybrid Engine Score

Jori Ford

Chief Marketing & Product Officer, FoodBoss

Hybrid Engine Optimization — a weekly, measurable scoring system that separates noise from signal across rankings, AI Overviews, and assistant retrieval.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

3:00 PM

3:30 PM

break

Afternoon Break

3:30 PM

4:00 PM

talk

Everything Is an Entropy Game: What Dies Between Retrieval and Citation

Metehan Yeşilyurt

Co-Founder & CGO, AEO Vision

A year reverse-engineering how ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI pick citations. AI doesn't reward quality — it rewards certainty. Entropy reduction is the new SEO.

  • →Entropy-based decoding in RAG is real
  • →Rerankers are confidence machines
  • →Information gain = next-gen reranking frontier
My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

4:00 PM

4:30 PM

talk

Is AI Seeing the Brand You Think You've Built?

Scott Stouffer

Co-Founder + CTO, Market Brew

AI doesn't retrieve brands by keywords — it retrieves by similarity. Measure your brand's mathematical identity and align your content to it.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

4:30 PM

5:00 PM

talk

From Showing Up to Winning: An IR & Systems-Level View of AI Search

Jeff Coyle

SVP Strategy, Siteimprove

Most AI visibility reporting measures appearance, not mechanism. Break AI search into the underlying pipeline — crawlability, rendering, semantic structure, retrieval, synthesis, citation reuse.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

5:00 PM

7:00 PM

social

Welcome Reception

Drinks, light bites, and conversations. Sponsored by LastMile Retail.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

Tuesday, April 28, 2026

Day 02 · The Psychology

Search is changing for users AND marketers. How AI, evolving consumer expectations, and new search experiences are reshaping how people find information and how brands need to think, create, and adapt.

8:00 AM

9:00 AM

registration

Registration & Coffee

9:00 AM

9:45 AM

keynote

SEO is a Performance Channel, GEO Isn't. How Do You Pivot?

Wil Reynolds

Founder & CEO, Seer Interactive

Why GEO and SEO play by different rules; how to find the prompts that reflect what customers actually ask; how to sell value (not quick wins) to budget holders.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

9:45 AM

10:15 AM

talk

SEOs Own Less Than We Think: The Influence Gap No One Trains You For

Bianca Anderson

Growth Strategist, Stellar Search Signals

AEO/GEO work increasingly lives outside SEO's direct control. A constraints-first framework for moving stalled cross-functional initiatives forward.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

10:15 AM

10:45 AM

break

Morning Break

10:45 AM

11:15 AM

talk

The Death of the Middleman: Why 'Good Enough' AI is Killing Traditional Marketing

John Doherty

Founder, JFD Coaching

AI commoditized 'good enough' execution. Smaller, faster, more profitable teams that lean into Empathy, Ethics, and Taste win. From Doer to Architect.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

11:15 AM

11:45 AM

talk

Run Persona Run: A Year in the Life of Me, Myself, and AI

Garrett Sussman

Director of Marketing, iPullRank

Year-long experiments on how persona cues, seeded context, and connected data change brand recommendations across AI Search.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

11:45 AM

meal

Lunch

1:15 PM

1:45 PM

talk

Navigating the Fractured Funnel & the Rise of Delegated Choice

Azeem Ahmad

Strategy Director, Reflect Digital

Default Effect and Status Quo Bias redefined by platform evolution. Generational divide in discovery and how to adjust tactics.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

1:45 PM

2:15 PM

talk

Managing the FUD Out of Change

Brittan King

Fractional CRO, iPullRank

Decidedly human session on navigating fear/uncertainty/doubt in a perpetually shifting paradigm. Practical tips for centering work amid chaos.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

2:15 PM

2:45 PM

talk

Trifecta Content — Pages That Drive Rankings, Visibility & Engagement

Amanda Milligan

Content & Growth Manager, Semrush

What 'content quality' means in the AI era and how to create content that packs the biggest punch in 2026.

2:45 PM

3:15 PM

break

Afternoon Break

3:15 PM

3:45 PM

talk

Quality Wins: How to Scale AI Content Without Losing What Makes It Work

Alex Halliday

Founder & CEO, AirOps

What data shows AI search actually rewards — brand context, humans in the loop, and performance checks. Three-part system the best content teams are building.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

3:45 PM

4:15 PM

talk

Teach The Sh*t Out Of Everything

Ian Lurie

The Digital Marketing Nerd, Ian Lurie LLC

Why teaching is a marketing superpower across every search surface. One teaching recipe that always works (except when it doesn't).

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

4:15 PM

4:45 PM

talk

I Have a Degree for This: Reclaiming Expertise in an AI-Mandated World

Angela Clark

Senior Content Strategist, iPullRank

Psychological tension between professional identity and AI mandates. Framework for strengthening human skills while leveraging AI tools.

social

Sponsor-hosted meetups across NYC. Sponsored by Yext, DemandSphere, AirOps.

Wednesday, April 29, 2026

Day 03 · The Ecosystem

SEO isn't one-size-fits-all. Enterprise search, vertical-specific strategies (video, local, e-commerce), and how to build scalable, future-proof search.

8:00 AM

9:00 AM

registration

Registration & Coffee

9:00 AM

9:45 AM

keynote

Inception: Plant Your Brand Into the Memory Layer of Every LLM

Ross Simmonds

Founder & CEO, Foundation

Every conversation with ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini shapes what AI believes and recommends. Audit what LLMs know about your brand; create 'inception moments'.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

9:45 AM

10:15 AM

talk

7 Small Creative Changes That Lead to BIG Discovery Wins

Carrie Rose

CEO & Founder, Rise at Seven

Combining creative work with technical SEO — examples from JDsports, Red Bull, Ninja, Airalo, and Kroger.

My Notes

10:15 AM

10:45 AM

break

Morning Break

10:45 AM

11:15 AM

talk

Inside the LLM Black Box: How to Gain AI Visibility

Brie Moreau

Founder, White Light Digital Marketing

1,000+ hours of R&D analyzing 2M+ AI citations across ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini. Which signals are real, which are noise, why authoritative sites get ignored.

My Notes

Notes will appear here once published.

Open session

11:15 AM

11:45 AM

talk

Watch the Party Die: Engineering 'Answerability' for Prospective Students

Ray Martinez

VP of SEO, Archer Education

Archer's playbook for AI Web Search + LLM Fetch. Student-first knowledge graph with custom schema, entity gap analysis, semantic scoring.

talk

What TikTok Shop Can Teach SEOs About Content That's Seen and Converts

talk

Your Competitors Are Still Prompting. You Could Be Building.

Principal, BrainDo

Thursday, April 30, 2026

Day 04 · The Future

Search is being rewritten in real time. Conversational search platforms, the evolution of Google, and the rise of alternative engines.

9:00 AM

registration

Registration & Coffee

10:00 AM

10:30 AM

talk

Beyond the Click: How to Shape What AI Says About Your Brand

James Cadwallader

Co-Founder & CEO, Profound

SEO and AEO can coexist. AEO ensures favorable appearance in AI; sentiment, positioning, and accuracy as ROI levers.

My Notes

10:30 AM

11:00 AM

talk

Two Studies, 38 Million Data Points & the Blind Spot in Every AI Search Study

Christian Ward

Chief Data Officer, Yext

Elo chess ranking on 21.6M local results + 17.2M AI citations across 4 models. Why analyzing AI visibility brand-down misreads the journey.

talk

talk
